name: Runtime Signal Reopen Context
summary: Human disconfirmation arrives as an external context change rather than as a direct approval-veto escalation, and the runtime must still reopen the work instead of preserving an implicitly green review state.
expected_reviewer_behavior: treat the external signal as a real human disconfirmation source
reopen the work into clarification instead of accepting the prior review state
preserve the changed constraint in a human-auditable artifact chain
failure_if: signal-driven reopen is flattened into a generic routing detail
the prior green state survives without explicit reopen evidence
